title: Generating Allorejection-Resistant Universal CAR-NKT Cells and Evaluating Their Efficacy
author: Fang, Chen, et al.

steps.title: Before you begin
steps.instruction: The clinical-guided method of generating the allogeneic CAR-NKT cells for off-the-shelf cancer immunotherapy with genetically engineered hematopoietic stem and progenitor cells and feeder-free differentiation culture was detailed discussed in the work by Li et al.2 In this paper, we shifted the focus on the comprehensive in vitro and in vivo assays to evaluate the efficacy, mechanism of action, safety, and immunogenicity of the UCAR-NKT (including Allo15BCAR-NKT and U15BCAR-NKT) cells.1,3

steps.title: Before you begin
steps.instruction: For full details on protocol use and execution, please refer to Li et al.3

steps.title: Quantification and statistical analysis
steps.instruction: A Prism 8 software (GraphPad) was utilized for all statistical analysis. Pairwise comparisons were performed with a 2-tailed Student’s t test. Multiple comparisons were performed with an ordinary 1-way ANOVA, followed by Tukey’s multiple comparisons test. Unless otherwise indicated, data are presented as the mean GSEM. In all figures and figure legends, ‘‘n’’ represents the number of biological replicates in which the experiment was performed. A P value of less than 0.05 was considered significant. ns, not significant; ∗p < 0.05; ∗∗p < 0.01; ∗∗∗p < 0.001; ∗∗∗∗p < 0.0001.
